name: review-fix-converge
description: For code review, security audit, or architecture review tasks. Cycles review→fix→verify until convergence.

steps.name: Review
steps.what: Perform cumulative-scope review of the codebase. Spawn harness-review-agent. Focus on: correctness, security, performance, maintainability. Write findings to .claude/harness/logs/review_report.json.
tools: Read → read source files in review scope
Grep → search for patterns and anti-patterns

existence: Report contains findings array with severity levels
quality: Finding density >= 1 finding per 500 LOC (or explicit justification for sparse modules)
Every 'no issue' claim backed by evidence of what was checked
Report includes blind spot / coverage gap acknowledgment section

steps.failure_handling: If review_agent fails to produce report, retry once with narrower scope. If density check fails, re-dispatch with 'go deeper' instruction.

steps.name: Fix
steps.what: Apply fixes for ALL findings from .claude/harness/logs/review_report.json. Before fixing, infer the convergence dimensions from the task description — identify which finding categories must reach zero (e.g., severity levels like P0/P1, quality checks like lint/type errors, or any criteria the user expects fully resolved). At minimum, P0 and P1 findings must be fixed. If the task mentions additional quality criteria, those are also mandatory. No finding may be deferred or skipped. Each fix must reference the finding ID it resolves.
tools: Bash → run syntax checks after fixes

existence: All findings in the inferred convergence dimensions have corresponding code changes
Each code change references the finding ID in commit or comment
quality: Fixes address root cause, not symptoms
Existing tests still pass after fixes

steps.failure_handling: If fix introduces new errors, revert and retry with different approach. Max 3 retries per finding.

steps.name: Verify
steps.what: Spawn harness-eval-agent to validate that all fixes are correct and no regressions introduced. Determine the appropriate verification command from project context — check package.json scripts, Makefile targets, pyproject.toml, or other build system configs. If the task description specifies a verification method, prefer that. Eval-agent checks against numbered standards in eval-criteria.md including convergence criterion.
tools: Bash → run the verification command inferred from project context
Read → verify file changes match fix descriptions

existence: Eval report produced with PASS/FAIL per standard
quality: Convergence criterion passes: all inferred convergence dimensions = 0
No regressions in previously passing tests

steps.failure_handling: If eval fails, feed failure details back to Fix step in next cycle. If circuit breaker trips, report convergence failure summary.

cycle.convergence_inference: Analyze the task description to identify what quality dimensions must reach zero for convergence. Look for: severity levels mentioned (P0, P1, P2), quality tools referenced (lint, type-check, test runner), or phrases like 'all issues', 'clean build', 'zero warnings'. Default to P0 findings and P1 findings if the task description doesn't specify.
cycle._comment_convergence: This inference logic is shared with other cycle templates. Changes must be applied to all.

eval_standards.name: Finding Density
eval_standards.check: Review finding density meets minimum threshold
eval_standards.method: Count findings per module/dimension in review_report.json. Calculate LOC / finding ratio.
eval_standards.pass_condition: Each module has >= 1 finding per 500 LOC, OR review explicitly justifies sparsity with evidence.
eval_standards.on_fail: Re-dispatch reviewer with instruction to go deeper on sparse modules

eval_standards.name: Exhaustion Evidence
eval_standards.check: Every 'no issue' claim is backed by evidence
eval_standards.method: Scan review_report.json for patterns: 'no issues', 'looks fine', 'appears correct'. Verify each has supporting evidence.
eval_standards.pass_condition: Every module/dimension with 0 findings includes specific evidence of what was checked and why it is genuinely clean.
eval_standards.on_fail: Re-dispatch reviewer to re-examine dimensions lacking evidence

eval_standards.name: Convergence
eval_standards.check: All inferred convergence dimensions reach zero across review cycles
eval_standards.method: Compare review_report.json findings between current cycle and previous cycle. If cycle < min_cycles, automatically FAIL.
eval_standards.pass_condition: cycle_iteration >= min_cycles AND all inferred convergence dimensions resolved to 0 AND review includes evidence section explaining what changed between cycles.
eval_standards.on_fail: Continue to next cycle. If cycle >= max_cycles, output convergence failure summary.

eval_standards.name: Blind Spot Acknowledgment
eval_standards.check: Review acknowledges its own blind spots
eval_standards.method: Check review_report.json for a 'blind_spots' or 'coverage_gaps' section.
eval_standards.pass_condition: Review includes section listing: areas intentionally excluded (with reason), areas needing deeper analysis next iteration, known gaps in this review round.
eval_standards.on_fail: Append blind spot analysis before proceeding

eval_standards.name: Fix Completeness
eval_standards.check: ALL findings across inferred convergence dimensions from latest review have been addressed
eval_standards.method: Cross-reference review_report.json findings with actual code changes. For each inferred convergence dimension, verify zero unresolved findings. Each fix references the finding ID it resolves.
eval_standards.pass_condition: Zero unresolved findings in every inferred convergence dimension. Each fix references the finding ID it resolves.
eval_standards.on_fail: Return to Fix step to address remaining findings
